title: Clearwater Build
description: Designed by naval architect Cyrus Hamlin, the Clearwater was built at the Harvey Gamage shipyard. The keel laying occurred in 1968.
subject: wooden boats; Pete Seeger; the Clearwater; sailing

title: Boat work
description: The builders followed Hamlin's precise specifications to create an authentic Hudson River sloop.
subject: wooden boats; the Clearwater

title: Pete Seeger Performance
description: In celebration of the boat's construction, Pete Seeger visited Harvey Gamage shipyard in 1968 and performed for a small crowd.
subject: Pete Seeger; environment; the Clearwater; New England

title: Clearwater Launch
description: On May 17, 1969, the Clearwater launched from South Bristol, Maine.
subject: Pete Seeger; environment; the Clearwater; New England

title: Maiden Voyage
description: The crew sailed along the Atlantic Coast, performing concerts at towns along the waterfront.
subject: Pete Seeger; the Clearwater; maiden voyage;

title: Clearwater Sail
description: In April 1970, the Clearwater sailed past a junkyard in the Hudson Valley. That month, the boat went to Washington, DC, for the first Earth Day
subject: Pete Seeger; environment; Maine

title: Clearwater Festival
description: Spearheaded by Toshi Seeger, the Great Hudson River Revival was officially inaugurated in 1978. It built upon the momentum of the smaller fundraising concerts that Toshi and Pete started in the 1960s, becoming a multistage eco-friendly extravaganza.
subject: Toshi Seeger; environmentalism; Great Hudson River Revival; folk music

title: Pete Seeger's Banjo
description: Pete Seeger's iconic banjo bears the words, "This machine surrounds hate, and forces it to surrender."
subject: Pete Seeger; banjo; folk music

title: Quite Early Morning
description: In 2012, Pete Seeger performed the song Quite Early Morning at Manhattanville College.
subject: Pete Seeger; banjo; folk music

title: Pete Seeger Thinking
description: In this image, taken at the Beacon Sloop Club, Seeger recollected his experiences traveling the country with Woody Guthrie in the 1940s.
subject: Pete Seeger; Woody Guthrie; Hudson River

title: The Woody Guthrie
description: Built in 1978, the Woody Guthrie is a smaller version of the Clearwater that sails around the Beacon waterfront, raising awareness about local environmental concerns and offering free sails to the community.
subject: Woody Guthrie; Pete Seeger; Beacon Sloop Club

title: Boat work
description: In 2012, the Clearwater crew commenced a massive rebuild of the boat.
subject: The Clearwater; boat restoration

title: Revival
description: Despite a massive overhaul, the boat made back in the water for the Great Hudson River Revival. In this image, Pete Seeger performs the closing ceremony along with Joanne Shenandoah.
subject: Pete Seeger; Joanne Shenandoah
